title: ADP vs Lever
description: Compare ADP vs Lever in July 20. We analyzed features, pricing, user ratings, and real-world performance to help you choose the right hr & people tool.
verdict: After thorough evaluation, Lever emerges as our recommendation for most users with a 4.2/5 rating compared to 4/5. However, the best choice depends on your specific needs. We recommend evaluating both tools against your requirements.

faqs.question: Which is better, ADP or Lever?
faqs.answer: Based on our detailed comparison, Lever edges ahead with a 4.2/5 rating. ADP excels at ADP is the world's largest payroll and HR services provider, processing payroll , while Lever is stronger for Applicant tracking system and recruiting platform..

faqs.question: What are the main differences between ADP and Lever?
faqs.answer: ADP and Lever differ in pricing (Custom vs $80-120/mo), target audience, and feature focus. Our comparison table breaks down these differences.

faqs.question: Is ADP cheaper than Lever?
faqs.answer: ADP starts at Custom while Lever starts at $80-120/mo. Consider total cost of ownership including implementation and training.

faqs.question: Which tool has better features, ADP or Lever?
faqs.answer: Both platforms offer strong features for hr & people. Lever scores higher overall, but specific feature needs may favor one over the other.

faqs.question: Can I use ADP and Lever together?
faqs.answer: Many organizations use both tools for complementary purposes. However, for overlapping use cases, choosing one streamlines workflows.

faqs.question: Which is easier to use, ADP or Lever?
faqs.answer: Both ADP and Lever prioritize user experience. The best choice depends on your team's technical expertise and workflow preferences.

faqs.question: Which platform has better customer support?
faqs.answer: Both offer documentation and support tiers. The quality varies by plan level — check our detailed review for support comparisons.

faqs.question: Which hr & people tool is best for my team size?
faqs.answer: Enterprise features favor ADP for large teams, while Lever may be more suitable for smaller teams with its Custom pricing.
